hidden_gems.name: Davide Spillare
hidden_gems.address: Via Scandolara 7, 37030 Brognoligo di Monteforte d'Alpone, Italy
hidden_gems.classification: Soave Classico DOC / Lessini Durello DOC
hidden_gems.description: Young Garganega producer in the Soave Classico volcanic hillsides fermenting without additions and using extended skin contact on his Pico cuvee. A benchmark name for natural-leaning Soave.
hidden_gems.why_locals_love_it: Spillare trained under Angiolino Maule of La Biancara and brought his low-intervention philosophy to the Soave Classico sub-zone; his wines circulate mostly via the VinNatur network and natural wine bars in Verona.
hidden_gems.tip: Look for the Pico skin-contact Garganega and the Durella sparkling; rare bottles and small production mean you need to buy direct or via specialist importers.

hidden_gems.name: Daniele Piccinin
hidden_gems.classification: Valpolicella Classico DOC / Amarone della Valpolicella DOCG
hidden_gems.description: A tiny estate in Fumane making organic Valpolicella and Amarone by hand with no added sulfites in the cellar. Piccinin's wines are structured and earthy, beloved by the Italian natural-wine community.
hidden_gems.why_locals_love_it: Piccinin's family has farmed in Fumane for generations; his wines rarely appear outside specialist Italian merchants and VinNatur channels. Verona-based sommeliers count Piccinin as the reference for honest, unsulfited Valpolicella.
hidden_gems.tip: The Valpolicella Classico is the entry point; ask at Verona wine bars like Osteria del Bugiardo whether they stock a current vintage.

hidden_gems.name: Monte dall'Ora
hidden_gems.address: Loc. Castelrotto, Via Monte dall'Ora 5, 37024 San Pietro in Cariano, Italy
hidden_gems.neighborhood: Valpolicella Classico, San Pietro in Cariano
hidden_gems.classification: Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC / Amarone della Valpolicella DOCG
hidden_gems.description: One of Valpolicella's few Demeter-certified biodynamic estates, run by Carlo and Alessandra Venturini in Castelrotto. Long-maceration Valpolicella and Amarone with no fining or filtering; the wines age particularly well.
hidden_gems.why_locals_love_it: Monte dall'Ora's Amarone Stropa is considered one of the most authentic expressions of biodynamic Valpolicella; the estate is not widely marketed internationally, making it a genuine insiders' find.
hidden_gems.tip: The Saseti Valpolicella Classico Superiore is the accessible entry wine; visit the estate in Castelrotto for a hillside tasting with Alessandra.

verified.address_quoted: Via Monte dall'Ora 5, Castelrotto, 37024 San Pietro in Cariano

hidden_gems.name: Filippi
hidden_gems.address: Via Pavia 36, 37030 Castelcerino di Soave, Italy
hidden_gems.description: Filippo Filippi farms the steep Castelcerino hillside vineyards of Soave Classico using organically certified viticulture. His wines are made with native yeasts and minimal sulfur, exploring the volcanic and limestone soil variations across the Castelcerino, Montefoscarino and Turbiana sub-zones.
hidden_gems.why_locals_love_it: Filippi's sub-zone single-vineyard Soave wines are among the most site-specific expressions in the appellation; Italian wine journalists repeatedly cite his Castelcerino as a benchmark for age-worthy Garganega.
hidden_gems.tip: Look for the Castelcerino label: grown on the 1971 pergola Veronese trained vines on calcareous hillside soils. Very limited production.

hidden_gems.name: Casa Coste Piane
hidden_gems.address: Coste Piane 2, 31049 Santo Stefano di Valdobbiadene, Italy
hidden_gems.classification: Prosecco Superiore DOCG / Rifermentato in Bottiglia
hidden_gems.description: Loris Follador's three-hectare estate in Santo Stefano di Valdobbiadene is the reference point for col fondo (cloudy, pet-nat style) Prosecco. His 'Fontanasanta' Rifermentato in Bottiglia has been the standard-bearer for traditional method Prosecco and is produced in tiny quantities.
hidden_gems.why_locals_love_it: Casa Coste Piane is the wine that made natural Prosecco a serious category. Follador has been making col fondo for over two decades; his wines are available only through specialist natural wine merchants and VinNatur.
hidden_gems.tip: Serve the col fondo lightly chilled and without decanting; the cloudy bottle is intentional and the lees add texture and complexity.

verified.address_quoted: Coste Piane 2, 31049 Santo Stefano di Valdobbiadene

hidden_gems.name: Cantina Giardino
hidden_gems.neighborhood: Campania (wines sold in Veneto natural wine scene)
hidden_gems.description: While based in Campania, Cantina Giardino wines are a constant presence at Veneto natural wine bars and at VinNatur in Monteforte d'Alpone. The Vino in Rivolta skin-contact Fiano and natural Aglianico are cult favourites among Verona's sommelier community.
hidden_gems.why_locals_love_it: Verona's natural-wine bars regularly feature Cantina Giardino as their 'cross-regional discovery' pour; the wines demonstrate how the Veneto natural wine movement has opened a pan-Italian discourse that goes beyond local appellations.
hidden_gems.tip: Ask for the Segreta white blend at Enoteca Segreta in Verona or at the VinNatur fair; rare outside the natural-wine circuit.

hidden_gems.name: Costadila
hidden_gems.address: Via Barch 2, 31049 Rolle di Cison di Valmarino, Italy
hidden_gems.description: Ernesto Cattel and Adelaide Tommasi run this small estate in Rolle di Cison di Valmarino producing col fondo Prosecco from heritage Glera and traditional varieties on steep hillsides with old vine material. Their 280 slm and 450 slm altitude labels are reference-point natural Prosecco.
hidden_gems.why_locals_love_it: Costadila is one of the handful of estates that kept the col fondo tradition alive when Charmat-method Prosecco dominated. The 450 slm altitude cuvee in particular is sought after by collectors of natural Italian sparkling.
hidden_gems.tip: The numbers (280, 450) refer to altitude in metres above sea level; the higher the altitude, the more mineral and taut the wine.

verified.address_quoted: Via Barch 2, 31049 Rolle di Cison di Valmarino

hidden_gems.name: Giovanni Ederle
hidden_gems.description: A family estate in Montebello Vicentino making Gambellara Classico from volcanic basalt soils at the base of the Berici Hills. Gambellara DOC is the little-known sibling of Soave, also Garganega-based but with distinct volcanic character; Ederle's Classico is among the most honest expressions of the appellation.
hidden_gems.why_locals_love_it: Gambellara is consistently undervalued relative to Soave; local wine lovers consider Ederle's Classico the most transparent expression of the volcanic terroir. Sells mostly to local restaurants and at the VinNatur fair.
hidden_gems.tip: Gambellara Vin Santo (the dried-grape dessert wine version) is also produced by Ederle; a very rare style worth seeking at the estate.
